title: Reading a gene, ORF to protein
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Hand it a stretch of DNA and it does what a molecular biologist does first: find the open reading frame (ATG … stop), translate it through the genetic code, and profile the sequence — GC content, melting temperature, codon count. This synthetic construct hides a 20-codon ORF between short UTRs. From bases to amino acids in one deterministic pass — the reading step behind every gene annotation.
